paper_title: Relevance of Airborne Allergenic Pollen in Indoor Environments

methods.description: The process of retrieving and selecting relevant literature on airborne allergenic pollen in indoor environments from various scientific databases to identify research gaps and derive recommendations for future research.

steps.description: The data presented in this brief review on the relevance of airborne allergenic pollen in indoor environments was retrieved from the databases of the Royal Chemical Society, ScienceDirect, Wiley, and Clarivate using the string of key words: 'indoor pollen' or 'pollen taxa' or 'indoor air quality' or 'health effects' or 'perennial allergy' or 'pollen metabolites' or 'statistical assessment' or 'pollen count'.
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve literature data from specified databases using keyword search

steps.description: The time period of the literature review was chosen from 2012-2022 to reflect the current evolution of research in this field during the last decade, to identify research gaps, and to derive recommendations for future research directions.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter retrieved documents by publication date from 2012 to 2022

steps.description: From the search results, original research articles were selected for further analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Select original research articles from filtered documents

methods.description: The process of categorizing information extracted from the literature survey to address the research question on the relevance of airborne allergenic pollen in indoor environments.

steps.description: The literature review is focused on the research question: 'What is the relevance of airborne allergenic pollen in indoor environments?' To answer this question, the information extracted from the literature survey was grouped into the following five categories: Information on the studied area and the building type (WHERE), Information on the investigation period (WHEN), Information on the pollen speciation (WHAT), Information on the number concentration (HOW MUCH), and Information on the observed allergy symptoms of the building occupants (WHY).
steps.searchable_summary: Categorize extracted information into specified categories
